title: Delicate Peppery British Tofu Ploughmans
description: A peppery and fluffy British dish featuring silken tofu, perfect for a date night, with a shojin-friendly and ploughmans form. This dish is made with long-fermentation-dough and silken tofu for a unique taste experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the long-fermentation-dough bread into 1-inch thick slices and toast until crispy.
In a small bowl, whisk together the apple cider vinegar and olive oil.
In a separate bowl, mix together the cubed silken tofu, ground peppercorns, and pickled onions.
Arrange the toasted bread slices on a platter or individual plates.
Top the bread slices with the mixed greens, cherry tomatoes, and tofu mixture.
Drizzle the apple cider vinegar and olive oil dressing over the top.
Garnish with additional peppercorns and pickled onions if desired.
